behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is about deterministic key-generation logic at a single function/object boundary. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess is required. LLM-DEP and MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Rejection gate: Integration/System are disqualified because the behavior text only asserts key derivation correctness from query parameters, with no module handoff or external dependency requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Test the key-generator function directly with a table of parameter sets: identical inputs (including reordered maps/arrays if supported) must produce identical keys; distinct inputs must produce distinct keys within the supported input domain.

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule (e.g., user profiles = 5 minutes, product catalog = 1 hour).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: core failure is rule-mapping/calculation logic, which is local. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP and MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Rejection gate: Integration is disqualified because behavior text focuses on TTL computation by category rule, not configuration-loading or cross-module propagation.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test TTL computation with injected in-memory freshness rules and fixed time reference; assert exact TTL outputs per category and default/fallback handling if specified.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU as specified in the configuration.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at module boundaries (memory-pressure signal + config + strategy selector), not just one function. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required if memory usage input is provided by an in-process monitor abstraction; (b) no AI/ML dependency; (c) no multi-agentic chain. Rejection gate: System is disqualified because behavior text only requires switching based on threshold/config, which can be reproduced via in-process module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Wire memory monitor, config provider, and eviction-strategy manager in-process. Drive usage below/above threshold and verify selected policy changes from LRU to LFU exactly at configured boundary, including hysteresis behavior if defined.

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly depends on real PostgreSQL write semantics and cache invalidation reaction path. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing DB writes can hide failures in SQL triggers/listeners/transaction timing/notification plumbing. LLM-DEP and MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Rejection gate: Integration is disqualified by the concrete text 'write to the underlying PostgreSQL table', which requires validating the real DB boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run against a real PostgreSQL instance and real cache layer. Seed rows and corresponding cache entries, execute table writes (insert/update/delete as applicable), then assert all matching cache keys are invalidated after commit and non-matching keys remain.

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is algorithmic policy correctness for eviction ordering, typically within a local cache/policy object. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP and MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Rejection gate: Integration is disqualified because behavior text targets policy semantics (LRU/LFU ordering) rather than inter-module or external-system interactions.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test cache/policy object with deterministic access patterns to force capacity overflow. Assert evicted key is exactly LRU in LRU mode and LFU in LFU mode, including tie-break rules if specified.
